items.title: Cyclone Narelle devastates WA tourist town, airport 'obliterated'

children.content: The northern WA tourist town of Exmouth has been left isolated and heavily damaged after Cyclone Narelle tore through the town.

items.title: WA minister wants reforms to local council laws

children.content: Local Government Minister Hannah Beazley says reforms are needed to prevent local councils from quitting just a few months into the job.

image.altText: A woman is seen looking into the camera while holding a book

items.title: Remote WA area connected to outside world with first at-home internet service

children.content: Residents from remote Ngalingkadji in Western Australia's north are being connected to the internet at home for the first time, bringing banking services, telehealth and emergency services.

image.altText: a town sign with tires

items.title: 'Like a war zone': Exmouth picks up the pieces in the wake of Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle weakens as it travels south through WA's Wheatbelt, leaving a trail of destruction in its wake.

articles.title: Track Cyclone Narelle's path and live wind speeds in WA

children.content: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle and monitor live wind speeds as it crosses the WA coast.

image.altText: An illustration shows a cyclone icon above the Kimberley coast in the north of Western Australia

articles.title: WA gas facilities, ports suffer major disruptions after cyclone

children.content: Gas giants Woodside, Santos and Chevron confirm they have suffered production outages at its gas as a result of Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle.

image.altText: An aerial shot of the Gorgon gas project on the coast of Western Australia.

gallery.altText: A tree uprootes and concrete slab distrubed at a school.

thumbnail.altText: A tree uprootes and concrete slab distrubed at a school.

items.title: Knights take control against Bulldogs after shock ambush

children.content: The Knights were heavy outsiders against Canterbury but have started fast and hold a handy lead over the Bulldogs. Follow live.

image.altText: Sandon Smith throws a pass during a match

items.title: Dockers battle Tigers after Lions flex premiership muscle to beat Saints

children.content: Fremantle plays host to Richmond with one eye on the weather in Perth, while Brisbane has too much run and too much class for St Kilda in a 33-point win. Follow live.

image.altText: Shai Bolton handballs while being tackled by Sam Lalor

items.title: EPA, developer defend records over contentious WA beach development

children.content: The City of Busselton says the watchdog has ignored its own rules after giving the Smiths Beach development the green light, but Fini Group says its work was "rigorous".

gallery.altText: An overhead shot of a beach with white sand, blue water and lots of people.

thumbnail.altText: An overhead shot of a beach with white sand, blue water and lots of people.

items.title: 'All the villages will be deserted’: The crisis emptying Greek islands and towns

children.content: Greece's population could drop by as much as 20 per cent by 2050, with the government declaring the situation a "national threat".

items.title: Lambs hit $500 as supply dwindles towards record low

children.content: Australian sheep and lamb prices keep rising despite global uncertainty. But how long will consumers be willing to pay?

image.altText: A pen full of lambs.

items.title: Head of TGA speaks out on sunscreen scandal

children.content: In his first interview since the sunscreen controversy erupted almost 10 months ago, the Therapeutic Goods Administration head says he hopes the proposed shake-up of regulations will restore consumer confidence.

image.altText: Two tubes of sunscreen with 50+ SPF showing.

items.title: PM leaves door open to halving fuel excise temporarily

children.content: The prime minister leaves the door open to halving taxes on petrol and diesel, after the Coalition demands temporary relief from sky-high fuel prices.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese, wearing a suit and glasses, standing in front an Australian flag with his hand on his stomach.

items.title: Trump's new tourism envoy once proposed banishing pigeons from Sydney streets

children.content: US President Donald Trump has named Nick Adams as his new special presidential envoy for tourism. It isn't the Australian's first time in government.

image.altText: Nick Adams and Donald Trump stand in front of American flags.

items.title: Do we actually have the workforce for the $4b NDIS alternative?

children.content: Months away from the start of a huge shift in disability support for children, the workforce expected to deliver it has concerns about its readiness.

gallery.altText: An over the shoulder photo of a young girl's hands playing with putty

thumbnail.altText: An over the shoulder photo of a young girl's hands playing with putty

items.title: 'Built on deception': How one online travel site is fleecing customers

children.content: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit pulls a suitcase through an airport.

items.title: Dementia is Australia's top killer, yet most still miss the early signs

children.content: Dementia is the leading cause of death in Australia and stigmas around the disease can delay diagnosis. But some in the community are at work to break taboos.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a hand resting on an elderly woman's hands.

items.title: End of an era for general store owned by family for 100 years

children.content: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

image.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.

items.title: Newlyweds sell everything to buy 160-year-old former pub

children.content: A 160-year-old hotel sat on the market for four years, until Jason and Kyla Romaior decided to take a risk. Insurance costs are $20,000 a year, but they are confident they have what it takes to handle the challenges of owning a heritage building.

image.altText: couple leaning over fence on verandah of wooden building, smiling

items.title: Daily Perth–Rome flights introduced by Qantas as Middle East routes collapse

children.content: Qantas will increase capacity on flights between Australia and Europe after Easter in a move that may see the "kangaroo route" evolve — and the airline is also boosting its Perth–Singapore service to 10 flights a week.

gallery.altText: the tail of a Qantas Boeing 767
A screenshot of FlightRadar24, showing no planes over the Middle East and Ukraine and lots of flights over Central Asia.

thumbnail.altText: the tail of a Qantas Boeing 767
A screenshot of FlightRadar24, showing no planes over the Middle East and Ukraine and lots of flights over Central Asia.

avatar.alt: A white woman with long blonde hair sitting in a TV studio and smiling

items.title: Three killed in a spate of road crashes across north and central Queensland

children.content: Queensland has recorded three new traffic fatalities over the past 24 hours. There were 64 fatalities recorded in the state from January 1 to March 22, compared to 49 in the same period last year. 2025 marked a 16-year high for road deaths.

image.altText: Police tape in the foreground with trees and car in the background

items.title: 'Gigantic waves' batter NSW coastline amid 'treacherous' surf conditions

children.content: Huge swells have inundated NSW beaches as most of the coastline remains closed to the public due to hazardous surf conditions.

image.altText: The Sandon Point Surf Life Saving Club building with large waves seen in the distance.

items.title: AFL players to lose insurance coverage for brain injuries

children.content: More than 500 AFL players will soon no longer be covered by insurance for brain injuries in their superannuation after Zurich Insurance moved to cut their ability to claim for concussion and head trauma.

image.altText: A football on the ground during Hawthorn Hawks' training session at Waverley Park in 2008.

items.title: Tiger Woods arrested following car crash near Florida home

children.content: Golf legend Tiger Woods is arrested in Florida after refusing to submit a urine sample following a car crash near his Florida home.

image.altText: Golfer Tiger Woods stands by his overturned vehicle

items.title: Inflation set to soar as fuel costs hit prices of everyday items from bread to ridesharing

children.content: Fuel surcharges are being applied across a range of industries, from the delivery of bricks to building sites to Australia's biggest rideshare companies, meaning consumer prices are rising.

gallery.altText: A man in a grocery store smiles at the camera.
A van parked in front of a business.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a grocery store smiles at the camera.
A van parked in front of a business.

items.title: Is government intervention keeping LNG exporters on their 'best behaviour'?

children.content: With war in the Middle East throwing energy markets into chaos, Australian gas prices are strikingly subdued. Experts say looming intervention by Canberra may explain why.

image.altText: A ship docked in the ocean near Darwin.

items.title: AFL admits crucial umpire error late in Crows' defeat to Cats

children.content: Geelong's Tom Atkins convinced umpires a free-kick was his in a decision the AFL has since clarified should have gone Adelaide's way in a tight contest.

image.altText: Tom Atkins and Zac Taylor contest a ball on the ground near their feet

items.title: At 104, George is still setting the tempo as he closes in on world record

children.content: WA musician George Franklin has already achieved a milestone by turning 104. He could soon garner another: the record for being the world's oldest band conductor.

image.altText: An elderly man holds his arm up in front of an orchestra

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Jet fuel shortages, headbutting whales and an iconic image

children.content: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

image.altText: A photo of the sun shining on vibrant green hill under a bright blue sky dotted with clouds.

items.title: Review calls for overhaul of WA's 'strictest' guardianship secrecy laws

children.content: People living under a guardianship arrangement in WA can't publicly speak up, but a review says this has to change.

gallery.altText: A collage of three photos of people with their faces scratched out.

thumbnail.altText: A collage of three photos of people with their faces scratched out.
